extract: Meyer suggests the following stages for journalism to approach science: collection — data gathering through scientific method; storage — comprising the organization and forms of data storage; retrieval — presupposes consulting data made available by third parties and offering ways of consultation; analysis — combining statistical tests and other ways of investigating patterns in relation to a topic; reduction — selection of what is maintained in the analysis and what will not be addressed; communication — data presentation, which in DDJ usually occurs through the use of charts.
chapter: Ways of working: method and workflows
themes: workflow; data pipeline; scientific method; Meyer
relevance_rationale: Meyer six-stage scientific journalism workflow is a foundational reference model that persists conceptually

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The Escola de Dados developed its own methodology — the so-called Data Pipeline — to organize the stages that form a method: ask, find, obtain, verify, clean, analyze, and present. The main difference in relation to Meyer's proposal concerns the inclusion of a question as the starting point for obtaining data related to the topic to be addressed.
chapter: Ways of working: method and workflows
themes: workflow; data pipeline; question-driven; methodology
relevance_rationale: Escola de Dados pipeline endures conceptually but needs updating to include LLM-assisted steps in ask-find-obtain-verify-clean-analyze-present

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The Deutsche Welle Data-Driven Journalism team presents its own method for finding stories in databases. In general terms, the procedures involve: defining a main question or hypothesis, finding the data, verifying the source, understanding the data, cleaning the data, analyzing the data, analyzing the obtained results, visualizing the data, validating the results, and, finally, supplementing this work with reporting techniques.
chapter: Ways of working: method and workflows
themes: workflow; hypothesis; verification; validation; data pipeline
relevance_rationale: DW pipeline endures as process model but verification and analysis steps now incorporate LLM-assisted validation

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The proposals presented here are still insufficient with regard to transparency and data openness, essential to the scientific process on which DDJ is based. The proposals are also limited in terms of checking information, whose numbers are usually interpreted literally by journalists when presented by official sources, but not always exhaustively verified.
chapter: Ways of working: method and workflows
themes: verification; transparency; methodology gaps; official sources
relevance_rationale: Critique that existing pipelines lack sufficient transparency and verification steps remains valid and even more relevant with AI

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Transparency in journalism corresponds, broadly speaking, to the process of opening up information from the journalistic organization and the processes it develops. At the organizational level, it presupposes the disclosure of funding sources and error-correction policies; at the operational level, it explains, for example, how a news piece was produced, why the topic matters, what sources were consulted, what the results of the analysis are, and the existence of any limitations.
chapter: Transparency as the opening of method and editorial decisions
themes: transparency; methodology; organizational; operational; accountability
relevance_rationale: Transparency at both organizational and operational levels as journalism ethics principle is permanent

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The journalist needs to be a filter, as well as a transmitter; an organizer and interpreter, as well as one who gathers and delivers facts. The competency of writing well would no longer be sufficient.
themes: skills; data literacy; journalist role; filtering
relevance_rationale: Journalist as filter and interpreter endures but LLMs now assist with filtering and organizing, changing the required competency mix
notes: Attributed to Philip Meyer

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The sources and the method used must be transparent so that other journalists or experts can repeat the same procedure under the same conditions. They must also arrive at the same result.
chapter: Transparency as the opening of method and editorial decisions
themes: transparency; reproducibility; verification; scientific method
relevance_rationale: Transparent methods enabling reproducibility by other journalists is a foundational scientific journalism principle
notes: Attributed to Sponholz

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Rigor is going to the bottom of the materially achievable investigations, in the time given for this research. It is the collection of all confirmed available facts. It is everything that opposes falsification, distortion, and lying.
chapter: Transparency as the opening of method and editorial decisions
themes: rigor; verification; truth; methodology
relevance_rationale: Rigor as exhaustive investigation opposing falsification and distortion is a timeless journalistic value
notes: Attributed to Cornu

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The emergence of practices like DDJ is closer to the promise of giving journalism a scientific character. They warn, however, that the authority lies not in simply showing the numbers and winning over audiences through their precision, but in the possibility of numbers contributing to the creation of a more rigorous discipline of verification. Data, therefore, demand greater methodological transparency.
chapter: Transparency as the opening of method and editorial decisions
themes: verification; transparency; scientific method; data journalism
relevance_rationale: Data demanding greater methodological transparency to support verification discipline is permanently true
notes: Attributed to Kovach and Rosenstiel

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Journalists place excessive trust in statistics provided by supposedly apolitical official sources, such as the United Nations and smaller non-governmental organizations. He concludes that journalists hide behind their sources, rarely verifying or questioning the numbers presented. The uncritical use of evidence of evidence allows journalists to protect themselves from criticism if they use incorrect statistics.
chapter: Transparency as the opening of method and editorial decisions
themes: verification; official sources; critical thinking; statistics; accountability
relevance_rationale: Critique of uncritical trust in official statistics and hiding behind sources remains deeply relevant
notes: Attributed to Lawson (2021)

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Philip Meyer stated that readers deserve not just the facts, but the truth of the facts: to guarantee the reliability of information, journalists need to use belt and suspenders, making an analogy to the need for double-checking.
chapter: Transparency as the opening of method and editorial decisions
themes: verification; fact-checking; reliability; double-checking
relevance_rationale: Double-checking as belt-and-suspenders reliability practice is a permanent verification principle

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The best journalism describes its methods with such clarity and precision that the reader is convinced that they could follow the same procedures and reach the same conclusions.
chapter: Transparency practices in DDJ newsrooms (Brazil and Germany)
themes: transparency; replicability; methodology; credibility; verification
relevance_rationale: Methods described with enough clarity for replication is the gold standard for transparent journalism, unchanged by AI

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: In the case of DDJ, access to the original databases would allow the audience and other reporters to conduct their own analyses and judge the validity of the conclusions derived from a given journalistic piece.
chapter: Transparency practices in DDJ newsrooms (Brazil and Germany)
themes: transparency; open data; verification; replicability
relevance_rationale: Public access to original databases enabling independent analysis and validation is a permanent transparency principle

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The Markup Method, according to editor Julia Angwin (2021), consists of the detailed publication of the methodology used in the reports. There are three main steps toward the scientific method: the elaboration of original data, peer review by specialists, and the communication of the data.
themes: methodology; peer review; transparency; scientific method
relevance_rationale: The Markup Method of publishing methodology with peer review steps is a model that endures and gains importance with AI

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: For transparency to sustain itself as an operator of an ethical practice and a discursive resource of legitimation, it must, strictly speaking, be an element that resonates both at the organizational level and at the operational level — in this case, it must also be reflected in the product.
chapter: Transparency and its levels: organizational and operational
themes: transparency; ethics; organizational design; credibility
relevance_rationale: Transparency as ethical practice must resonate at organizational and operational levels -- a permanent governance principle

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The methodology employed in a Data-Driven Journalism report typically appears at the end of the text, in the form of a methodological note. This is a section that seeks to translate a series of procedures carried out by reporters to ensure clarity regarding the scope of data selection and collection, as well as to justify choices such as the adoption of certain sources and procedures.
themes: methodology; transparency; documentation
relevance_rationale: Methodological notes explaining scope, sources, and procedural choices are a permanent DDJ transparency practice

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Traces of reporting refer to journalistic records that normally remain behind the scenes and are not exposed in published texts, but that help readers gain an overview of the difficulties involved in the reporting process. They differ from methodology in that they are more specific and subtle; they indicate, for example, how many times the journalist called a given source or contacted a press office, how many databases had to be searched to find a particular topic or analytical value, as well as errors found in the original documents, among other problems.
themes: transparency; reporting process; documentation; verification
relevance_rationale: Traces of reporting showing behind-the-scenes difficulties help readers understand the full investigation context

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: In addition to the possibility of describing the working method only in narrative form, one of the distinguishing features of DDJ is precisely organizing its methods in code format and, in this way, enabling the reproducibility and verification of information. Thus, other journalists and newsrooms are able to verify the content produced, as in a kind of peer review, and eventually reuse the code.
themes: reproducibility; open source; peer review; verification; code
relevance_rationale: Code-based reproducibility endures but now must also account for LLM prompts, model versions, and non-deterministic AI outputs

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The proposal depicted in Figure 32 presents a series of steps for journalistic work. It starts from a problem (1) and a hypothesis (2) for which information must be sought through research (3), and whose databases present a context (4) about how they were produced and where they are situated. After examining the feasibility of using the information found, the process moves to the stages of collection (5) and analysis of information (6), which require the elaboration of lines of open source code (7) for subsequent reproducibility.
chapter: Proposed Method with a Focus on Transparency and Reproducibility
themes: methodology; pipeline; transparency; reproducibility; hypothesis
relevance_rationale: Pipeline starting from problem and hypothesis endures but open-source code requirement now extends to LLM prompt logs

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The insertion of information into a spreadsheet or other type of document involves an initial human effort and is therefore subject to mistakes. Thus, data must be treated as any documentary source and, for this reason, must be evaluated in their totality. I argue that the context of data must be studied as soon as the journalist finds the evidence they intend to work with.
themes: data quality; verification; source criticism; context
relevance_rationale: Treating data as documentary source requiring full evaluation of context and provenance is permanently essential

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: While replicability presupposes the use of code for new analyses, or could even refer to a methodology explained narratively, reproducibility involves the execution of step by step, allowing for the complete verification of results and their validation, similar to the premises adopted in open science.
themes: replicability; reproducibility; open science; verification
relevance_rationale: Distinction between replicability and reproducibility endures but must now address non-deterministic LLM outputs

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: When spreadsheet software is used for data analysis, formulas, filters, and pivot tables are generally employed — processes that tend to remain hidden in the results. When the analytical stages are structured in a programming language, the tools allow the step-by-step to be commented and documented, making the procedures clear and accessible so that they can be replicated or reproduced later.
themes: reproducibility; code; spreadsheets; documentation; transparency
relevance_rationale: Spreadsheet analysis hides steps while code documents them -- true but LLMs now generate code, adding a new transparency layer to manage

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Data collection must be related to the problem and the hypothesis proposed at the beginning of the method, and therefore may involve combining different sources. Data collection is not always simple and direct — in some government portals, for example, data are made available in closed formats (such as .pdfs, which make it difficult to extract information). In these cases, we say that the data are available, but not accessible.
themes: data collection; open data; scraping; accessibility
relevance_rationale: Data collection from government portals with closed formats endures but LLMs can now assist with PDF extraction and format conversion

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: The formulation of a hypothesis is central to scientific investigation. It functions as a kind of guide for the researcher, directing focus toward specific questions to be answered, and for the reader, who has a kind of plan and set of premises for understanding the results presented.
themes: hypothesis; scientific method; methodology
relevance_rationale: Hypothesis formulation as central to scientific investigation guiding researcher and reader is a permanent methodological principle

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Especially at the beginning of the pandemic, the availability and organization of data related to Covid-19 presented gaps and problems, such as underreporting of cases. In this way, data journalists needed to seek alternatives. Upon finding databases that presented numbers about the pandemic, a kind of explanation about the uncertainties and limitations of the databases needed to accompany the reports.
themes: uncertainty; data quality; limitations; transparency
relevance_rationale: Communicating uncertainty, limitations, and underreporting in data-driven stories is a permanent transparency obligation

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Whether public or developed within a journalistic organization, a dataset must be accompanied by comprehensive documentation so that the audience is able to understand how the information was collected and how it is presented. The sources, in turn, present their own context of production and the limitations inherent to the process.
themes: documentation; data biography; context; transparency
relevance_rationale: Comprehensive dataset documentation explaining collection context and limitations is a permanent best practice

source_title: Os Elementos de Transparência no Jornalismo Guiado por Dados
author: academic
extract: Data analysis requires multiple precautions. It is first necessary to verify whether the figures form part of a dataset that exhibits a normal distribution. Numbers that do not belong to this orderly distribution are called outliers and, although journalism has a tradition of reporting singular cases, a single absolute or isolated number is rarely able to explain a phenomenon.
themes: statistics; outliers; analysis; data quality
relevance_rationale: Verifying normal distribution, checking for outliers, and not relying on single absolute numbers are permanent analytical principles

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: data journalism research has traditionally focused on a limited set of countries, primarily within the liberal and democratic corporatist media systems. Many recently published studies illustrate a growing scholarly interest in data journalism in other parts of the world, but these studies are still limited in number.
themes: comparative research; geographic bias; media systems; research gaps
relevance_rationale: Geographic bias in data journalism research is a structural issue unaffected by LLM tools

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: tools and practices from Western countries may guide the journalistic epistemological approach to truth and knowledge as well as the study of such practices and expertise in other parts of the world, yet with different outcomes
themes: epistemology; Western bias; knowledge production; data journalism practice
relevance_rationale: Recognition that Western tools shape epistemological approaches globally is a critical insight independent of AI

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: without the development of a body of research about how data journalism is being practiced and/or hindered in non-Western contexts, there is a risk that it will contribute to a widening of the cultural divide.
themes: cultural divide; Global South; research gaps; inclusion
relevance_rationale: Risk of cultural divide widening without non-Western research applies equally in the AI era

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: the specific contexts of these countries limit the generalizability or comparability of these findings to journalists working in other countries and media systems.
themes: generalizability; comparative research; media systems
relevance_rationale: Limits on generalizability across media systems is a methodological concern unaffected by technology

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: even when open access or transparency regulation is in place, this does not guarantee that data journalists will have broad access to information. Next to legislation, a culture of accountability is needed to guarantee that journalists have access to information.
chapter: Journalistic cultures, media markets, and the political environment
themes: open data; transparency; FOI; data access; accountability
relevance_rationale: Open data legislation without accountability culture is insufficient for data access regardless of AI capabilities

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: Access to data might not mean the same as usability as data, which, for instance, can be unstructured, paired with restrictions of use, or impossible to download in bulk. Future research should look into how such limitations, on the one hand, hinder data journalism beyond the Western world, but on the other hand may also lead to new forms of data journalism to circumvent these limitations.
chapter: A maturing field of global data journalism research
themes: data quality; data access; data usability; innovation
relevance_rationale: LLMs can help structure unusable data and work around format restrictions, partially addressing data usability barriers

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: the goals of civic tech are not the simple diffusion of Western ideas but rather a negotiation of local and national origins with global issues. The two practices that civic tech and journalists share are those of facilitating and gatekeeping, with facilitating being about enabling others to take action themselves and gatekeeping being about highlighting information that is deemed publicly relevant.
themes: civic tech; collaboration; gatekeeping; participation
relevance_rationale: Civic tech as negotiation of local and global issues through facilitating and gatekeeping is a durable framework

source_title: Data Journalism Research: Studying a Maturing Field
author: Stalph, Hahn et al.
extract: journalistic ideals typically associated with the liberal media model rub on to how data journalism develops as a practice across the globe regardless of media systems. Future research should therefore critically examine how journalistic ideas related to the liberal model affect the data journalistic practice in other media systems.
chapter: A maturing field of global data journalism research
themes: liberal model; post-colonialism; research agenda; media systems
relevance_rationale: Critical examination of how the liberal media model shapes global DJ practice is a structural research question unaffected by AI

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: systematic reviews in social sciences are called for when a general overall picture of the evidence in a topic area is needed to direct future research efforts
themes: systematic review; research methodology; evidence synthesis
relevance_rationale: The need for systematic evidence synthesis to direct research efforts is a methodological principle independent of AI tools

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: Traditional literature reviews, unlike systematic ones, lack rigorous, transparent and replicable explicit methodology. Systematic reviews are quite important in helping researchers to make sense of the ever-growing research literature on any particular topic. If the researchers choose to work based on the findings or evidence shared by a single case study, they run the risk of getting biased.
themes: systematic review; bias; transparency; replicability
relevance_rationale: The distinction between rigorous systematic reviews and ad-hoc literature reviews remains methodologically fundamental

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: we searched Google Scholar, Web of Science, Scopus, Science Direct, JSTOR & ERIC databases using the specific set of keywords as exact phrases and using the Boolean operator OR. The search query was: teaching data journalism OR data journalism education OR data journalism training.
themes: search strategy; databases; keywords; systematic review
relevance_rationale: LLMs can now assist with database searching and keyword expansion, though the systematic protocol design itself endures

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: In the second phase, studies citing these selected studies (as per Google Scholar indexing of citations) were examined separately and relevant studies which fell within the aforementioned criteria were selected. To make the selection of corpus for the review comprehensive and systematic, the reference list of the selected studies was examined to find any relevant study which was not yet part of the corpus.
themes: citation chaining; snowball search; corpus building; systematic review
relevance_rationale: Citation chaining and snowball searches can now be partially automated by AI tools like Semantic Scholar and Elicit, though human judgment on relevance remains

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: Thomas and Harden (2008) suggest a three-step approach to carry out the thematic analysis for systematic reviews of qualitative studies. In the first stage, the literature corpus is coded through a line-by-line reading. These codes are used for formulating descriptive themes and analytical themes at later stages.
themes: thematic analysis; coding; systematic review; qualitative methods
relevance_rationale: LLMs can now assist with line-by-line coding and theme formulation, though human interpretive judgment for analytical themes endures

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: a content matrix was created in tabular form where each column was devoted to the broader themes in the RQs (like challenges, methods of teaching, etc.) and also other bibliographic data for each publication. Coders placed the excerpts from the individual studies in the literature corpus in the relevant columns of the matrix, effectively isolating the relevant excerpts according to themes.
themes: content matrix; coding framework; thematic synthesis; data extraction
relevance_rationale: Content matrix creation and excerpt extraction can be accelerated by LLMs, though the thematic framework design requires human judgment

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: translation is the process of taking concepts from one study and recognising the same concepts in another study, though they may not be expressed using identical words. After this stage, the concepts and findings from different studies were summarised according to their similarities and differences to synthesise the findings of the review.
themes: concept translation; synthesis; thematic analysis; systematic review
relevance_rationale: LLMs excel at concept translation across studies using different terminology, though human validation of equivalence claims is still needed

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: data journalism training is an emerging field which demands an ensemble of skillsets and expertise to complete the workflow, especially in case of advanced projects. This aspect has raised a challenge in formulating an exact boundary to ascertain what skillsets to focus on and which tools to be included in the training.
themes: skills; curriculum design; interdisciplinarity; data journalism education
relevance_rationale: The challenge of defining DJ skill boundaries is intensified by LLMs which blur the line between journalist and analyst capabilities

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: use of project-based approach is a common method in data journalism teaching, with slighter variations in its implementation. This approach follows a teaching hospital method by engaging students to work on a data story project, with each of them ideally going through different steps in the production of a data story.
themes: project-based learning; pedagogy; data journalism education; workflow
relevance_rationale: Project-based learning and teaching-hospital pedagogy remain effective regardless of whether LLMs assist with individual pipeline steps

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: a major recommendation is to integrate lessons on basic numeracy into existing units of the programme, especially during the first year. Collaborative learning and hands-on practical sessions are also recommended as confidence-building measures by several scholars.
themes: numeracy; pedagogy; confidence building; curriculum design
relevance_rationale: Basic numeracy integration endures but LLMs can now scaffold confidence-building with on-demand statistical explanations

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: Most of the studies which were examined in the review consider data journalism education primarily as a means of imparting technological and management skills required to deal with datasets, rather than developing critical data literacy in students. data literacy is overly perceived as technical abilities like extracting data, making statistical analysis, creating visualizations, interpreting and reporting appropriately.
themes: critical data literacy; data literacy; pedagogy; epistemology
relevance_rationale: The critique that DJ education overemphasizes technical skills at the expense of critical data literacy is amplified, not resolved, by LLM availability

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: there is a widespread tendency in many studies to consider data or datasets as passive, value-free elements which are used by students to develop technological skills. This denial of the materiality of datasets as techno-social assemblages is problematic, since such an approach stops short of helping students to explore the role played by the techno-social data infrastructures which formulated the dataset and their influence on the meaning-making process.
themes: data materiality; critical data literacy; epistemology; data infrastructure
relevance_rationale: Recognizing datasets as techno-social assemblages shaped by power and infrastructure is a critical literacy that becomes more important with AI-mediated data

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: ethical aspects of data journalism were largely absent in the course materials reviewed by them and that data journalism educators lacked awareness about ethical data journalism practices. lessons about ethical practices are necessary in data journalism training as these enable students to understand the importance of accountability, transparency and privacy issues.
themes: ethics; transparency; privacy; accountability; data journalism education
relevance_rationale: Ethics education in DJ including accountability, transparency and privacy becomes more urgent as AI tools introduce new ethical risks

source_title: Teaching Data Journalism: A Systematic Review
author: various
extract: One major challenge with systematic reviews in social science disciplines like journalism studies is the existence of considerable amount of grey literature. Formulating a search protocol to systematically include relevant grey literature available in the form of industry reports or non-peer-reviewed research reports brought out by academic institutions was beyond the scope of this study.
themes: grey literature; systematic review limitations; search protocol
relevance_rationale: The challenge of grey literature in systematic reviews persists and is arguably compounded by proliferating AI-generated content

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: SLRs offer advantages over traditional literature reviews, which tend to be limited to literature already known to the authors or found through cursory searches. By contrast, SLRs require researchers to use broader search strategies, predefined search strings, and uniform inclusion and exclusion criteria, which can help to uncover studies outside of their typical areas of research and networks.
themes: systematic review; methodology; search strategy; inclusion criteria
relevance_rationale: The advantages of SLRs over cursory literature reviews -- broader searches, predefined criteria -- are methodological principles unchanged by AI

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: an initial search was conducted in the WOS Core Collection using data journalism and data-driven journalism as keywords to identify relevant studies. Keywords related to earlier forms of quantitative journalism were omitted since data journalism is not solely considered an evolution of those fields. The search spanned from December 1, 2008, to December 31, 2022, marking 2008 as the starting point because that is when the term data journalism was first used.
themes: search strategy; keywords; Web of Science; time interval
relevance_rationale: LLMs can now assist with keyword identification and database searching, though the protocol design and time-interval rationale remain human decisions

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: A coding guideline based on prior familiarity with data journalism scholarship was developed, covering article and author details, theoretical approaches, and methodological approaches. A pilot test involving a random sample of ten manuscripts validated the coding guidelines and predetermined categories for clarity and effectiveness. A coding log documented the rationale of categorization, encountered challenges, and their resolutions.
chapter: Full Text Review: Coding and Analysis
themes: coding guidelines; pilot testing; inter-coder reliability; systematic review
relevance_rationale: LLMs can accelerate coding guideline application and pilot testing, though the initial framework design and coding log discipline endure

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: data journalism focuses on using data as a primary source for storytelling and reporting. Additionally, it embraces participatory practices by involving the public in data collection, analysis, visualization, and storytelling. Furthermore, it advocates for open data policies and promotes the release of government or institutional datasets to the public.
themes: data journalism definition; participation; open data; storytelling
relevance_rationale: The definition of DJ as data-sourced storytelling with participatory and open data commitments remains a valid conceptual foundation

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: Verification, cross-checking from various sources, contextualizing the data, including the investigation methodology, and protecting sources are, in turn, the ethical standards of the practice.
themes: verification; cross-checking; ethics; source protection; methodology
relevance_rationale: Verification, cross-checking, contextualization, source protection, and methodology transparency are ethical standards undiminished by AI

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: data journalism's dependence on readily accessible, pre-processed public data from official institutions and non-commercial organizations and easy-to-use or freely available software solutions, is considered an obstacle to its development.
themes: data access; official data dependence; software tools; barriers
relevance_rationale: LLMs lower the barrier to working with pre-processed data and free tools, partially mitigating this obstacle though structural access issues persist

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: incomplete, outdated or error-filled data, inconvenient file formats, such as scanned copies of texts or PDF files containing tables, and the lack of digital/data literacy are significant challenges faced by data journalists worldwide. However, these challenges are more severe in the Global South where the lack of open data infrastructure and right-to-information legislation hampers data journalists' work.
themes: data quality; file formats; data literacy; Global South; FOI
relevance_rationale: LLMs can now extract tables from PDFs and handle inconvenient formats, partially addressing these challenges; Global South infrastructure gaps persist

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: data journalism research tends to focus more on practice than theory. only 47% of the corpus referring to a theoretical framework. The most commonly used theoretical frameworks are actor-network theory and field theory.
chapter: The Scholarship Lacks Strong Theoretical Framework and Methodological Diversity
themes: theoretical frameworks; actor-network theory; field theory; research gaps
relevance_rationale: The observation that DJ research favors practice over theory and lacks strong theoretical frameworks is a structural gap unaffected by AI tools

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: the methodological approaches of the studies, like their theoretical frameworks, needed more diversity and clarity. the consistent use of small samples from a single outlet or country poses a challenge to generalizing findings and building theories through comparative work. Moreover, the repeated focus on cases from North America and Europe risks reinforcing the problematic notion of a more advanced West.
chapter: The Scholarship Lacks Strong Theoretical Framework and Methodological Diversity
themes: methodological diversity; generalizability; Western bias; comparative research
relevance_rationale: The critique of small-sample Western-centric research methods is a structural academic problem unresolved by LLM capabilities

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: The most common methods are in-depth interviews (48 studies) and content analysis (31 studies). Other techniques such as surveys (13 studies), observations/newsroom ethnographies (9 studies), and document reviews (6 studies) are also frequently used. However, less common methods such as experiments (2 studies), participatory mapping (1 study), and scenario network mapping (1 study) are used at lower rates.
chapter: The Scholarship Lacks Strong Theoretical Framework and Methodological Diversity
themes: research methods; interviews; content analysis; methodological diversity
relevance_rationale: The inventory of common research methods in DJ scholarship documents the field and is not affected by LLM capabilities

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: Qualitative interviews can provide valuable insights into the processes and dynamics of creating data-driven news. However, more than these testimonies alone is needed to reveal whether there are discrepancies between interviewees' perceived roles and their actual performance. Therefore, researchers often combine interviews with other techniques, such as content or document analysis, to examine the observable attributes of the news.
chapter: The Scholarship Lacks Strong Theoretical Framework and Methodological Diversity
themes: triangulation; interviews; content analysis; mixed methods
relevance_rationale: The principle of triangulating interviews with content analysis to detect gaps between stated and actual practice endures as methodological rigor

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: postcolonial theory highlights the importance of engaging with diverse voices and perspectives and recognizing how power and privilege shape who has access to data and who can shape data-driven narratives. Furthermore, it can help critically examine how data is used to reinforce existing power structures and inequalities and identify strategies for using data to challenge and disrupt these structures.
chapter: The Scholarship Lacks Strong Theoretical Framework and Methodological Diversity
themes: postcolonial theory; power structures; data access; critical perspectives
relevance_rationale: Postcolonial critique of power structures in data access and narrative control becomes more urgent as AI tools risk further concentrating power

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: Scholars should consider utilizing triangulation and long-term explorations to understand data journalism in diverse contexts comprehensively. For instance, newsroom ethnographies can provide an in-depth understanding of the roles of peripheral actors, such as non-profits and civic technologists. Focus groups can also yield comprehensive and in-depth insights into attitudes, beliefs, experiences, and behaviors.
themes: triangulation; ethnography; focus groups; longitudinal research
relevance_rationale: The call for triangulation, ethnographies, focus groups, and long-term studies remains valid for understanding DJ in diverse contexts

source_title: Data Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Özlem Erkmen
extract: A holistic data journalism education should combine practical and theoretical content, encompassing both statistical and editorial skills, and encourage journalists to think in terms of data. Unfortunately, the lack of in-house professional development programs makes it challenging for data journalists working in the industry to acquire these skills.
