extract: It is not the increasing volume of data that makes data increasingly important, but the tools and ability of journalists to analyze it.
themes: tools; skills; analysis capability
relevance_rationale: The primacy of analytical skill over data volume remains true — LLMs amplify but do not replace judgment.
notes: David McCandless via Rogers and Gallagher 2013

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The barriers for entry have never been lower as free tools change the rules on who can analyse, visualize, and present data.
themes: open source; accessibility; tools; democratization
relevance_rationale: Barrier lowering has accelerated dramatically with LLMs making analysis accessible to non-coders.
notes: Rogers 2015

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The main reasons the media use open-source software rather than purchase proprietary software are: (1) When the prospect of reusing and reprocessing data is unclear, purchasing proprietary software requires capital and staff investment, while open-source software is free, easy to obtain, and simple to operate, effectively reducing the cost of investment. (2) The development of certain functions of proprietary software must be completed by the developer, which is costly, while open-source software can be designed by the media according to their own needs for specific functions. (3) Open-source software can already meet most of the needs related to data news production, so there is no need to purchase proprietary software.
themes: open source; cost; customization; tooling
relevance_rationale: Open-source rationale endures but the landscape now includes LLM APIs and AI-assisted coding tools alongside traditional OSS.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data-driven journalism can provide a more solid empirical basis for discussions about policy, economic trends, and social change.
chapter: Crisis of Trust in British and American Journalism
themes: empirical evidence; credibility; policy
relevance_rationale: Data journalism providing empirical grounding for policy discussions is a lasting public value.
notes: Brian Keegan via Howard 2014

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data can trigger hypotheses, it can disprove your hypothesis, but it always enhances a story that would otherwise be based on anecdotes.
chapter: Crisis of Trust in British and American Journalism
themes: hypothesis testing; evidence; verification
relevance_rationale: Data as hypothesis trigger and evidence enhancer is a timeless epistemological principle.
notes: Sandra Fish via Edge 2015

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data are everywhere all the time. They have something to say about us and how we live. But they aren't neutral, and neither are the algorithms we rely on to interpret them. The stories they tell are often incomplete, uncertain, and open-ended. Without journalists thinking in data, who will help us distinguish between good stories and bad?
chapter: The Crisis of Professionalism in Journalism
themes: data neutrality; algorithms; critical thinking; interpretation
relevance_rationale: Critical awareness that data and algorithms are not neutral is even more important with LLM-generated analysis.
notes: Mark Hansen via Bell 2012

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalism has changed the definition of news from 'the reporting of newly occurring facts' to 'the reporting of newly discovered facts.'
chapter: Discovering the Reality in the Data
themes: discovery; news definition; paradigm shift
relevance_rationale: Redefining news as discovery of facts in data persists regardless of the tools used for discovery.
notes: Shen Hao, Tsinghua University

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Alberto Nardelli, The Guardian's current data editor-in-chief, divides the paper's daily data journalism into three categories: quick and relevant numbers, data investigations to support stories, and making complex issues.
chapter: Multiple Models of Data Journalism Production
themes: production tiers; complexity levels; Guardian
relevance_rationale: Production tiers from quick numbers to deep investigations remain a valid organizational framework.
notes: Reid 2015

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: In the endogenous model, the traditionally newsroom-centered working model is broken. News production requires cross-border integration in which people in various positions have to understand each other from the beginning stage. This has made programmers, who used to be downstream of the news production line and often did some support work, key or even central.
themes: cross-disciplinary teams; programmers; integration
relevance_rationale: Cross-disciplinary integration endures but the role of programmers shifts when LLMs handle much of the coding.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: In Facts are Sacred, Simon Rogers refers to the 'short-form of data journalism' in The Guardian's daily data journalism production, where journalists very quickly identify key data, analyze it as the story is happening, and guide the audience to participate in it.
themes: short-form; speed; audience participation
relevance_rationale: Short-form data journalism concept endures but LLMs now enable even faster identification and analysis of key data.
notes: Rogers 2015

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The Guardian has templates with a choice of chart styles, color palettes, and interactive visualizations that journalists can use to produce data news quickly, and the BBC and Financial Times have similar ones.
themes: templates; visualization; speed; standardization
relevance_rationale: Templates endure conceptually but are now supplemented by AI-generated charts and automated visualization tools.
notes: Dick 2014

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalism competes on teamwork, not team size. News editors should bring data reporters in at the beginning of the story so that they can start identifying what needs to be developed for the project. Programmers need to judge the difficulty and cost based on the data journalist's choice of topic and research questions.
themes: teamwork; collaboration; planning; editorial workflow
relevance_rationale: Teamwork over team size and early collaboration between editors and data reporters remains essential.
notes: Au 2016; Huang and Zhang 2016

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: For example, when we check the domestic rocket launch records, the data provided by these authorities, such as China Academy of Launch Vehicle Technology, China Great Wall Industry Corporation, and China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation, are not the same, then we will decide not to write the news related to this.
themes: data verification; conflicting sources; editorial judgment
relevance_rationale: Verifying conflicting data sources before publishing is a timeless editorial practice.
notes: In-depth interview, Editor A

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Training has been incredibly impactful in the U.S. to spread data journalism, there's hardly a newsroom in the U.S. that doesn't have a specialist in this area, and training is a huge reason for that.
themes: training; skills development; newsroom capacity
relevance_rationale: Training to spread data journalism capacity in newsrooms remains critical, now including AI literacy.
notes: Aron Pilhofer via Reid 2014

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: One of the problems with data journalism production is that it's hard to assemble teams that are cross-disciplinary and have the right combination of designers, developers, graphic artists, photographers.
themes: team composition; cross-disciplinary; hiring
relevance_rationale: Challenge of assembling cross-disciplinary teams persists but required roles are shifting — fewer pure coders, more AI-literate journalists.
notes: Aron Pilhofer via Reid 2014

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The potential risk is that the quality of the data and content collected by crowdsourcing is inconsistent, and implementers will have to weigh the pros and cons if they are to crowdsource, as they have no control over the quality of this data. In this case, the team has to make sure that the data is properly verified.
themes: crowdsourcing; data quality; verification
relevance_rationale: Crowdsourced data quality risks and verification requirements persist regardless of era.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The crowdsourcing of data journalism is suitable for articles that seek breadth and diversity of coverage rather than depth and historicity of reporting. It is suitable for collecting data that is relevant to the public interest and more intuitive but struggles to show advantages when reporting on specialized issues and collecting authoritative data.
themes: crowdsourcing; scope; limitations; data collection
relevance_rationale: Crowdsourcing suited for breadth over depth remains a valid scoping principle.
notes: Fang 2015

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: It is important to design crowdsourcing tasks in a playful way. In The Guardian's crowdsourcing of politicians' election spending scandals, the newspaper included game mechanics in the crowdsourcing, where users could see which challenges were accepted and completed, which was used to give the users a sense of achievement and being rewarded.
themes: crowdsourcing; gamification; engagement; design
relevance_rationale: Gamification principles for crowdsourcing engagement are tool-agnostic design insights.
notes: Simon Rogers; Vehkoo 2013

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The media should not view each crowdsourcing participant as a one-time resource, but rather build a long-term relationship with them.
themes: crowdsourcing; community building; audience relationships
relevance_rationale: Building long-term relationships with crowdsourcing participants is a durable community strategy.
notes: Vehkoo 2013

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The process of statistical analysis generally consists of data collection, data cleaning, and data analysis. Data collection is the acquisition of data based on research questions, while data cleaning is the discovery and correction of identifiable errors in data files, including checking data consistency, dealing with invalid and missing values, and so forth.
chapter: Insight into Reality: Data Collection and Analysis
themes: pipeline; data cleaning; error correction; consistency
relevance_rationale: Pipeline of collection-cleaning-analysis endures but LLMs now automate much of the cleaning and error correction.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalists' access to data can be broadly classified in four ways: open access, access by application, self-collection, and shared access.
themes: data acquisition; classification; sourcing
relevance_rationale: Four-way classification of data access methods remains a valid taxonomy.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Open data is favored by data journalists because it is easy to obtain, does not require payment, and does not involve complicated procedures such as licensing when producing, and only requires a clear explanation of the source of the data.
themes: open data; sourcing; attribution; licensing
relevance_rationale: Open data advantages for journalists persist regardless of analytical tools used.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: With advances in data collection technology, some data journalists are obtaining data from online platforms through web scraping. ProPublica's data story 'Dollars for Docs' focused on various payments to doctors by pharmaceutical companies, which are required by law to disclose these data on their websites. Unable to obtain the relevant data through normal channels, the reporter collected them through web scraping.
themes: web scraping; data collection; ProPublica
relevance_rationale: Web scraping remains relevant but LLMs now offer alternative extraction methods and can assist with scraper code generation.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: In countries such as the U.K. and the U.S., it has become common for data journalists to request relevant data from the government under the Freedom of Information Act, which is known as a FOIA request.
themes: FOIA; government data; information requests
relevance_rationale: FOIA requests as a data acquisition method are a legal/institutional practice unaffected by LLMs.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The ubiquity of sensors and their ability to quantify something abstract are the main reasons why journalists are employing them to gather data. Journalists need data that is not all available from official sources, which may withhold information, which encourages journalists to open up some other ways of collecting data.
themes: sensors; self-collection; data gaps; alternative sourcing
relevance_rationale: Sensor journalism for self-collected data remains a distinct method unaffected by LLMs.
notes: Fergus Pitt, Tow Center

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Assessment of data quality requires assessments on a number of dimensions. First, the data is free of error, or the correctness of data. Second, the completeness of data, which can be viewed from at least three perspectives: schema completeness, column completeness, and population completeness. Third, the consistency of data. Fourth, the believability of data, or the extent to which data is regarded as true and credible. Fifth, the appropriate amount of data. Sixth, the timeliness of data. Eighth, the accessibility of data.
chapter: Problems with the Quality of the Data
themes: data quality; completeness; consistency; timeliness; assessment
relevance_rationale: Multi-dimensional data quality assessment framework is timeless and applies equally to LLM-processed data.
notes: Lee et al. 2006

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: There have been cases in the United States where media outlets have damaged the reputations of individuals because of inadequate verification of the data they obtained. A story in The Texas Tribune had a local man branded as a criminal, but it turned out to be a problem with the state's crime database itself.
chapter: Problems with the Quality of the Data
themes: verification; data errors; harm; accountability
relevance_rationale: Case study illustrating harm from inadequate data verification is a lasting cautionary lesson.
notes: Bradshaw 2013

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: In late 2012, data journalist Nils Mulvad finally got his hands on veterinary prescription data that he had been fighting for seven years. But he decided not to publish the data when he realized that it was full of errors.
chapter: Problems with the Quality of the Data
themes: data quality; editorial judgment; kill decision
relevance_rationale: Editorial judgment to kill a story when data is unreliable is a timeless professional norm.
notes: Bradshaw 2013

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalists need to keep personal data properly in all aspects of data collection, analysis, and publication. Who is in control of the data? With whom can the data be shared? How should the data be protected and secured? News organizations should not only make sure that sensitive data is securely electronically stored, but also that journalists get appropriate training on how to avoid security risks and potential misuse of data due to increased sharing and partnerships.
chapter: Violations and Controversies Arising from Non-compliance with Ethical Norms
themes: data security; privacy; ethics; personal data; training
relevance_rationale: Data security, privacy, and proper handling of personal data are enduring ethical requirements.
notes: Colin Porlezza

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: There are three main types of data analysis methods. The first type, Descriptive Analytics, uses descriptive statistics and data visualization to describe the basic characteristics of data. The second type, Predictive Analytics, uses causal analysis, correlation analysis, and other methods to derive potential patterns, common patterns, or future trends based on past or current data. The third type, Prescriptive Analytics, combines a number of influencing factors based on known data to provide the best decision solution.
themes: descriptive analytics; predictive analytics; prescriptive analytics; taxonomy
relevance_rationale: Three analytics types endure as concepts but LLMs now dramatically expand predictive and prescriptive capabilities available to journalists.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The original context of the data refers mainly to the purpose and method for which the data were collected, and relates to the boundaries of data interpretation. The context of the news text refers to the intent of the news to use the data, and is related to the applicability of the data. Generally speaking, the better the fit between the original context of the data and the context set by the news text, the more accurate people's understanding of the data will be, and the more credible the reality characterized by the data will be.
chapter: Distortion of Meaning Due to Ignoring the Original Context of the Data
themes: context; recontextualization; data interpretation; credibility
relevance_rationale: Understanding original data context to avoid misinterpretation is a permanent analytical requirement.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: It is important to understand whether data are comparable and to be careful when normalizing data across time and from different organizations on the same topic.
chapter: Distortion of Meaning Due to Ignoring the Original Context of the Data
themes: comparability; normalization; time series; data quality
relevance_rationale: Data comparability and normalization cautions persist regardless of analytical tooling.
notes: Lesson from Washington Post racial tolerance map criticism

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Caixin's 'How far is a high-speed railway station from you' introduces a distance index, which is the ratio of the straight-line distance from the station to the city center and the radius of the built-up area. A smaller distance index means that the station is easier to reach. This makes the data comparable and makes it easier to draw reliable conclusions.
chapter: Distortion of Meaning Due to Ignoring the Original Context of the Data
themes: normalization; index construction; comparability; context
relevance_rationale: Index construction for comparability is a durable analytical technique.
notes: Caixin Data News Center, 2019 Data Creators Conference

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: There is a case where the unreasonable choice of the time range leads to a large bias or even distortion in the recontextualized data. A powerful example is the widely reported 'national crime wave' in the United States in 2015. If journalists examine a broader period of time, they will see that crime rates were higher almost everywhere in the U.S. a decade ago, and almost twice as high two decades ago.
chapter: Distortion of Meaning Due to Ignoring the Original Context of the Data
themes: time range; cherry-picking; bias; context
relevance_rationale: Cherry-picking time ranges to distort data is a timeless analytical error to guard against.
notes: Groskopf 2015

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Open data is for government agencies, not journalists. That is, most datasets are named, structured, and organized according to the agencies, not the journalists looking for stories. Journalists should collect and analyze data with a good understanding of the original context of the data to avoid misinterpretation.
chapter: Distortion of Meaning Due to Ignoring the Original Context of the Data
themes: open data; context; misinterpretation; data structure
relevance_rationale: Caution about open data being structured for agencies not journalists is a persistent challenge.
notes: D'Ignazio 2019

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The U.S. population grew by 80% between 1960 and 2015, and in fact if we go by the murder rate per 100,000 people, the 2015 figure is close to the lowest since 1960. A comparison of only the total number of people to draw conclusions is undoubtedly one-sided.
chapter: Unreliable Conclusions from Ignoring the Applicability of Analytical Methods
themes: per capita rates; normalization; misleading totals
relevance_rationale: Per capita rate normalization is a fundamental statistical literacy lesson that persists.
notes: Whitby 2016 critique of Guardian murder stats

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Canada is so sparsely populated that the total population of the region does not reach 100,000 people, and the data was selected only for 2014, a year in which there were three murders in the region, compared to zero in the previous three years.
chapter: Unreliable Conclusions from Ignoring the Applicability of Analytical Methods
themes: small samples; cherry-picking; per capita rates; statistical validity
relevance_rationale: Small sample size and cherry-picking errors are timeless statistical pitfalls.
notes: McMaken 2015; Mises Wire error

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Europe One Degree Warmer, produced by the European Data Journalism Network, analyzes more than 100 million meteorological data points. The journalists used two datasets in their analysis that could not be directly compared, so a reconciliation algorithm was used to allow historical comparisons to be made.
chapter: Unreliable Conclusions from Ignoring the Applicability of Analytical Methods
themes: reconciliation; data harmonization; incompatible datasets
relevance_rationale: Data harmonization across incompatible datasets is a durable analytical challenge.
notes: EDJNet 2019

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Correlation is not the same as causation. In data journalism, it happens that correlation is mistaken for causation due to poor recognition of logical relationships. Assuming that countries with higher gun ownership rates have more gun homicides, there could be several reasons: gun ownership breeds homicides; the presence of homicides causes more people to buy guns for self-defense; some other reason causes both; or it is just a coincidence.
themes: correlation; causation; logical errors; confounders
relevance_rationale: Correlation vs causation is a foundational statistical literacy principle that never becomes obsolete.
notes: Stray 2014

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Some seeming 'correlations' are just 'coincidences' and do not mean that there is true causality between the two variables. A change in two variables in the same direction may be caused by a change in a third variable. Forcing the one explanatory variable to explain all the variation in the dependent variable is itself arbitrary.
themes: spurious correlation; confounding variables; overfitting
relevance_rationale: Spurious correlation and confounding variables are permanent pitfalls in data analysis.
notes: Whitby 2014

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Another common logical error is to make linear long-term predictions about a non-linear phenomenon.
themes: prediction; linearity; logical errors
relevance_rationale: Warning against linear extrapolation of non-linear phenomena is a timeless analytical principle.
notes: FiveThirtyEight death penalty example

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: We believe that the quality of data analysis can be improved in two ways: improving journalists' data analysis ability and introducing experts into the production process.
chapter: The Path to Improving Data Analysis in Data Journalism
themes: skills; expert collaboration; quality improvement
relevance_rationale: Two paths to quality endure but LLMs now serve as a third path — AI-assisted analysis augmenting both journalist skill and expert input.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Unlike traditional journalism, which treats experts as sources, data journalism incorporates experts into the news production process, i.e., have them embedded in the editorial team as content producers.
chapter: Inviting Experts to Participate in the Production Process
themes: expert collaboration; embedded experts; team composition
relevance_rationale: Embedding domain experts as content producers in editorial teams is a durable organizational insight.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Experts can have two functions in data analysis. First, experts are gatekeepers for the entire process of data analysis -- journalists should discuss with experts and peers and listen to different opinions in order to improve data analysis and conclusions, rather than making decisions on their own. Second, experts are directly responsible for data analysis, equivalent to outsourcing the data analysis process.
chapter: Inviting Experts to Participate in the Production Process
themes: expert gatekeeping; intersubjective objectivity; outsourcing analysis
relevance_rationale: Expert gatekeeping and peer review of data analysis remain essential even with LLM assistance.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Most awarded works rely heavily on data collected by governments and corporations, resulting in data journalism that lacks a critical awareness of these data and constructs reality in the way governments and corporations understand it.
chapter: How Data Journalism Awards Winners Apply Data Science
themes: critical awareness; data provenance; government data; power
relevance_rationale: Critical awareness that award-winning work over-relies on government data framing is a lasting insight about power dynamics.
notes: Analysis of DJA 2013-2018 winners

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The methods commonly used by reporters include descriptive data analysis, exploratory data analysis, database or data warehouse, machine learning, and information retrieval. Of the 31 identifiable samples, 20 used descriptive and exploratory data analysis, and 9 had only descriptive data analysis.
themes: descriptive analysis; exploratory analysis; machine learning; methods taxonomy
relevance_rationale: Methods taxonomy holds but LLMs have dramatically expanded accessible analytical methods beyond descriptive and exploratory analysis.
notes: DJA 2013-2018 analysis

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: We classify the data processing complexity into four levels: low (directly present the original data), medium (describe numerical and distributional characteristics like mean, median, variance), relatively high (multiple statistical analysis methods or simple programming tools such as R and Python), and high (mathematical modeling, big data mining, or algorithm innovation). The average score of the samples is only 0.94, which indicates that many of the Data Journalism Awards entries are low in the level of data processing.
themes: complexity levels; professionalism; assessment framework
relevance_rationale: Complexity levels framework endures but LLMs lower the barrier to higher-complexity analysis, so the distribution should shift upward.
notes: DJA 2013-2018 analysis

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: There are two main ways for media to build their own databases. First, the media organize and clean open data sets into high-quality open databases. Second, the 'niche database': the media systematically integrates open data, public data, leaked data, and self-collected data based on research questions to create a more personalized, user experience-oriented database.
chapter: Building Database to Provide Personalized Services and Innovate Profit Model
themes: database building; data curation; niche databases; open data
relevance_rationale: Media building curated and niche databases is a durable strategic practice.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Self-built databases help the media build 'strong ties' with users through interactive design, authoritative data, and topics of public interest. As a kind of product, databases have various profit models: attention-based advertising, fee-based download of datasets, and user-oriented targeted services.
chapter: Building Database to Provide Personalized Services and Innovate Profit Model
themes: databases as products; revenue models; audience engagement
relevance_rationale: Databases as revenue-generating products with various profit models is a lasting business model insight.
notes: Texas Tribune, ProPublica Data Store, Thomson Reuters examples

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Unstructured data is more ubiquitous and accessible than structured data, allowing the media to cover more topics. Unstructured data are more 'honest' than structured data. The processing of structured data relies on statistical methods, which are inevitably biased in representing reality. However, unstructured data contains complete and continuous information and key details, and is more reliable and credible in representing reality.
chapter: Using Unstructured Data to Represent the Broader Social Reality
themes: unstructured data; representation; completeness; statistical bias
relevance_rationale: LLMs have fundamentally changed unstructured data processing — they now excel at extracting meaning from text, making this observation outdated.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: There are three main applications of machine learning in data journalism production. First, machine learning helps media do classification and prediction via supervised learning. Second, machine learning helps the media gain insight into the world via unsupervised learning that can autonomously find correlation between massive data and identify hidden structure. Third, machine learning helps media make decisions via reinforcement learning.
chapter: Using Machine Learning to Enhance Big Data Processing
themes: machine learning; classification; prediction; unsupervised learning; reinforcement learning
relevance_rationale: ML applications in journalism are now vastly expanded by LLMs, making this taxonomy incomplete without generative AI capabilities.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The effectiveness of supervised learning depends heavily on the reliability of the algorithm and the training data; if they are not reliable, the data results will be wrong. In an investigation of the Los Angeles Times powered by machine learning algorithms, the algorithm had a 24% error rate and required manual review.
chapter: Using Machine Learning to Enhance Big Data Processing
themes: machine learning; error rates; manual review; validation
relevance_rationale: Need for human verification of ML outputs with known error rates is even more important with LLM hallucination risks.
notes: Nahser 2018

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: In Doctors and Sex Abuse from The Atlanta Journal-Constitution, journalists used 50 crawlers to obtain more than 100,000 physician disciplinary documents from the U.S. health care system, then used machine learning to clean and analyze the documents and retrieve keywords involving sexual assault.
chapter: Using Machine Learning to Enhance Big Data Processing
themes: machine learning; web scraping; document analysis; classification
relevance_rationale: Crawling plus ML classification pipeline endures conceptually but LLMs now handle document analysis far more efficiently than 50 separate crawlers.
notes: Global Investigative Journalism Network 2017

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalism is an opportunity for journalism to re-professionalize itself. Data science methodology is the core of data journalism production, so the level of journalists' mastery of it determines the level of understanding the world through data.
chapter: Future Trends in the Application of Data Science to Data Journalism
themes: professionalization; data science; methodology; skills
relevance_rationale: Data journalism as re-professionalization through methodology mastery is a lasting argument.

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalism production is not a process in which meaning is generated automatically and the truth emerges naturally. For example, the algorithms which secretly contribute to data journalism appear to be 'objective,' but they are not. A researcher's unconscious cognitive bias may be expressed through the selection criteria chosen for the algorithm. Additionally, the cleaning phase of data is inherently subjective, as the data analysts would make decisions about what IVs and DVs will be counted or not.
themes: subjectivity; algorithmic bias; data cleaning
relevance_rationale: Subjectivity in algorithm design and data cleaning is a permanent epistemological insight, amplified by LLM prompt design.
notes: Warns that both algorithm design and cleaning involve subjective editorial choices

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Although the same data and algorithms will yield the same data results, the interpretation of data results will vary among different media outlets. Meaning is not simply given, but is socially constructed across a number of institutional sites and practices.
themes: interpretation; framing; objectivity
relevance_rationale: Same data yielding different interpretations across institutions is a lasting insight about social construction of meaning.
notes: Same data can yield divergent stories depending on institutional context

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: It has been argued that framing in data journalism consists of two phases. The first stage -- building logical structures -- is drawing conclusions from the data, and the second stage -- building forms -- is visualizing the data.
themes: framing; visualization; workflow
relevance_rationale: Two-phase framing model of logical structure then visual form persists as an analytical framework.
notes: Two-phase framing model: logical structure then visual form

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Our analysis of the Data Journalism Awards winners and other outstanding works reveals that the narratives and analyses of complex issues in data news stories are mostly organized around four dimensions: the temporal dimension, the spatial dimension, the social network dimension, and the data relationship dimension. Data news can be organized along one or several narrative dimensions.
chapter: Dimensions of Complex Narratives in Data Journalism
themes: narrative structure; complexity; temporal; spatial; network; correlation
relevance_rationale: Four narrative dimensions for complex data stories is a durable structural framework.
notes: Four-dimension framework derived from DJA winners analysis

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: In data journalism, time is a dimension that helps one construct a robust and intuitive framework to establish connections between events. The timeline in data visualization allows a clear and visual presentation of the chronological or cause-and-effect relationships between events. In the temporal dimension, moments and time periods appear in four main combinations: (1) a single moment; (2) a time period made up of multiple moments in series; (3) independent moments; and (4) independent time periods.
chapter: Temporal Dimension: Depicting Changes and Trends
themes: timelines; temporal analysis; visualization
relevance_rationale: Temporal dimension patterns for data visualization are tool-agnostic design principles.
notes: Four combinatorial patterns for temporal data presentation

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The choice of beginning and ending points is a process of meaning construction. While timelines can provide a great deal of information on the temporal dimension, they also 'simplify' the historical context of events. In particular, comparisons on the same timeline force different actors to be involved in the same history, since each actor may face different situations at the same point in time. The timeline allows some key information about the actors to be omitted, serving instead the subjective intention of the creator.
chapter: Temporal Dimension: Depicting Changes and Trends
themes: timelines; framing; editorial choice; context
relevance_rationale: Critical awareness that timeline start/end points construct meaning is a lasting editorial insight.
notes: Caution: timeline start/end points are editorial choices that construct meaning

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Maps are some of the most information-dense ways of communicating data. You can do a lot in a map because people already understand the fundamentals -- unlike, say, a scatterplot. The use of color-based segmentation and various markers on the map can clearly convey important information within this geographic space.
chapter: Spatial Dimension: Achieving Classification and Comparison
themes: maps; spatial visualization; information density
relevance_rationale: Maps as information-dense communication and their intuitive readability are enduring visualization principles.
notes: Len De Groot (LA Times) on maps as intuitively understood data formats

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Had the map been presented in a different way, for example, by reducing the scale of the map to make the dots appear dense, or by limiting the view of the map to the core of the city, or by increasing the diameter of the dots in the map so that they dominate the image, we are afraid the map would have presented a completely different meaning. So maps are not purely scientific; they are also aesthetic, religious, social, and political.
chapter: Spatial Dimension: Achieving Classification and Comparison
themes: maps; visual rhetoric; scale manipulation; framing
relevance_rationale: Scale, viewport, and marker size as rhetorical choices on maps is a timeless critical insight.
notes: Scale, viewport, and marker size on maps are rhetorical choices that change meaning

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: If the producer wants to compare information related to spatial distribution, the map allows the reader to draw conclusions in a visual way. However, if the producer wants to compare information that does not relate to spatial distribution, the map's narrative will be inefficient and may even be misleading.
chapter: Spatial Dimension: Achieving Classification and Comparison
themes: maps; chart selection; misleading visualization
relevance_rationale: Maps only efficient for spatially distributed data is a permanent chart selection principle.
notes: Maps only efficient for spatially distributed data; misapplied maps mislead

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: During the presidential elections, the U.S. media often used a map of the United States to show the elections in different states, using red and blue to show the areas won by Republicans and Democrats respectively, which seems objective but is actually misleading. Usually 'red states' are larger than 'blue states,' so readers can easily see a large area of red on the map that represents the Republican Party, but this does not reflect the true election results. For example, Montana is about 15 times the size of Vermont, yet both have almost the same number of electors.
chapter: Spatial Dimension: Achieving Classification and Comparison
themes: maps; election coverage; area distortion; misleading visualization
relevance_rationale: Area distortion in election maps misleading voters is a lasting visualization literacy lesson.
notes: Geographic area \!= political weight; standard maps distort electoral reality

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Social network analysis is not yet mastered by all journalists and editors, and second, social network analysis requires sufficient data. A complete social network diagram can show three levels of information: macro view, relationship view, and micro view. Data journalists do not necessarily need to use all three views in a single story. Which views are used depends largely on the content and purpose of the story.
chapter: Social Network Dimension: Revealing Relationships in Complex Networks
themes: social network analysis; macro micro views; skill gap
relevance_rationale: SNA framework endures but LLMs now enable more journalists to perform network analysis without specialized training.
notes: Three-level SNA framework: macro, relationship, micro; choose by story purpose

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Correlation is useful because it provides clearer perspectives than causality. Correlation analysis is significant in itself, and it also lays the foundation for studying the causal relationship. Sometimes, clues of causality are also implied in correlation analyses. News reporting cannot rely solely on correlations to provide audiences with explanations of social reality, because the task of news reporting is not only to tell people what happened but also to tell them why these things happened. The revelation of causes is often the discovery of causality.
chapter: Correlation Dimension: Exploring Correlations Among Variables
themes: correlation; causation; statistical reasoning; explanation
relevance_rationale: Correlation as foundation for but not substitute for causal explanation is a permanent epistemological principle.
notes: Correlation valuable but insufficient; journalism must also pursue causal explanation

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Narrative visualization approaches can be divided into an author-driven approach and a reader-driven approach. A purely author-driven approach has a strict linear path, relies heavily on messaging, and includes no interactivity. In contrast, a purely reader-driven approach has no prescribed ordering of images, no messaging, and a high degree of interactivity. Most examples of narrative visualization fall somewhere in between, balancing both elements. Therefore, narrative visualization includes three basic models: Martini Glass Structure, Interactive Slideshow, and Drill-Down Story.
chapter: Linear Narrative and Parallel Narrative in Data Journalism
themes: narrative structure; interactivity; author-driven; reader-driven
relevance_rationale: Author-driven vs reader-driven narrative spectrum and three models remain a valid visualization taxonomy.
notes: Segel & Heer taxonomy: three models on author-reader spectrum

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Rather than providing users with a raw data set, data journalists use interactive tools to provide an intuitive and visual interface that retains meaningful information and removes useless variables after judging the value of the variables in the data set.
themes: interactive databases; variable selection; user interface
relevance_rationale: Editorial judgment on which variables to surface in interactive tools is a durable design principle.
notes: Database narratives require editorial judgment on which variables to surface

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Making complex data understandable to users should be realized by helping them follow a sequence from the general (far view) to the particular (near view). The far view is typically the landing page of the app, focused on broad meaning and context. This page should have the national picture of the data with ranked examples. The near view is the page at the lowest level of abstraction, where the reader is looking at her own school, his own town, etc. It is the means through which readers will understand the whole by relating it to the example they understand best.
themes: information architecture; far-near view; news apps; ProPublica
relevance_rationale: General-to-particular information architecture for data apps is a lasting UX principle.
notes: Scott Klein (ProPublica): general-to-particular sequence for data apps

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Interactive narratives are centered on rules rather than neutral technologies. In newsgame-type data news pieces, rules are predetermined for the purpose of keeping the game on track, defining the game's storyline and scoring criteria. In database-type data news works, the permutations and calculations between different variables are set in advance. The rules are the invisible carriers of the author's will and pre-determine the connotation of the data news text.
chapter: The Storytelling Mechanism of Data Journalism
themes: interactivity; rules; editorial control; illusion of choice
relevance_rationale: Rules embedded in interactive narratives controlling meaning is a permanent critical insight about interactivity.
notes: Interactive tools give illusion of user freedom but embed author intent via rules

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The first level of procedural rhetoric in interaction narratives is to use game mechanics to draw the user into a particular interaction diagram, thereby directing the user's attention to a specific area by anchoring. The main techniques used include: (1) Default views provide an initial point of interpretation anchored to the default visual configuration; (2) Fixed comparisons present some information by default so that users can contrast this information with other values in the visualization; (3) Navigation bars and menus allow users to search for specific information to constrain the data depiction based on a user's preferences for certain information, which can be called filtering.
chapter: The Storytelling Mechanism of Data Journalism
themes: procedural rhetoric; default views; anchoring; filtering; interaction design
relevance_rationale: Procedural rhetoric techniques — default views, fixed comparisons, filtering — are lasting interaction design principles.
notes: Three anchoring techniques: default views, fixed comparisons, filtering via nav bars

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data visualization means that the reality represented by data and the intricate relationships between data are expressed through a combination of visual codes, a seemingly neutral process that actually achieves persuasive discourse production in an implicit way. Data visualization, because it is derived from 'data,' is given the aura of 'science' and 'objectivity.' In the eyes of the general audience, the 'translation' of data into data visualization is not a subjective creation, but a trustworthy and scientific knowledge production process.
chapter: The Relationship Between Word and Image
themes: visualization rhetoric; objectivity illusion; persuasion
relevance_rationale: Critical insight that visualization produces persuasive discourse under an objectivity aura is timeless.
notes: Visualization appears neutral but implicitly produces persuasive discourse

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Image domination, if abused -- in other words, if journalists take advantage of people's trust in data visualization and the belief that 'seeing is believing' -- can create discursive violence under the neutral veil of visual text. In data journalism, image domination can be used to distort the truth, intentionally or unintentionally, to prove one's own point of view, often by intercepting the Y-axis of a chart or by not following conventional design principles.
themes: visualization ethics; Y-axis manipulation; design conventions; trust
relevance_rationale: Warning about image domination, Y-axis manipulation, and trust exploitation in visualization is permanent.
notes: Truncated Y-axes and unconventional design exploit audience trust in visual data

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Word domination occurs when the visual codes of data visualization cannot accomplish basic representation. A bar chart without labels and legends is just a graph without any meaningful information. Word domination also occurs when there is uncertainty about the meaning of images. Due to journalistic demands for truth, accuracy, and objectivity, words are needed to summarize, explain, or confirm the meaning of images when data visualization may have multiple meanings or may not achieve the intended communication purpose.
themes: annotation; labeling; word-image relationship; ambiguity
relevance_rationale: Charts requiring textual anchoring through labels and legends is a fundamental visualization principle.
notes: Charts require textual anchoring (labels, legends, annotations) to fix meaning

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The most important aspect of data visualization is making sure 'visualizations are clarifying' the stories rather than simplifying them. Data journalism is not the same as bringing data analysis or visualization techniques directly into journalism. The narrative is still at the heart of data journalism. Paul Bradshaw argues that the core process of data journalism is data processing, and that its main task is storytelling.
chapter: Story Awareness: Enhancing the Attractiveness of News
themes: storytelling; visualization purpose; narrative centrality
relevance_rationale: Core data journalism insight persists regardless of AI tooling
notes: Haddad (Al-Jazeera) and Bradshaw: viz should clarify not simplify; story is primary

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Information is transformed into a story through five specific elements: setting, plot, characters, conflict, and theme, and all stories must contain all five of these elements. While many people talk about the importance of data storytelling, few people use data to tell stories.
chapter: Story Awareness: Enhancing the Attractiveness of News
themes: storytelling; narrative elements; data storytelling
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Citraro (Periscopic): five required story elements; most data pieces lack them

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data news works usually calls for a long period of time for its production, so the strength of data journalism lies not in timeliness, but in proximity. Establishing a relationship between data and the audience through proximity requires a great sense of relevance to the audience. In order to strengthen the connection between news content and users, data journalists should judge whether the topic is closely related to the audience in the topic selection stage, which kinds of data are important and relevant to the audience in the data collection stage, whether the analysis results meet the needs of the audience in the data analysis stage, and identify the common and personalized needs of the audience in the data visualization stage.
themes: proximity; audience relevance; topic selection; personalization
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Relevance check at every pipeline stage: topic, collection, analysis, visualization

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The idea of making news personal is something we can do so much better now with online news. People really, really like talking about themselves. And giving them an opportunity to talk about themselves will turn them toward news. Some data journalists also use databases or other interactive means that can really allow readers to 'find themselves' in the news story.
themes: personalization; engagement; interactive databases; audience
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Claire Miller (Trinity Mirror): personal relevance drives engagement

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Asking readers to supply information can sometimes cut down on their desire to use a news app. Past projects have shown that asking people to enter their Zip code automatically guarantees that a large chunk of people won't use it while using geolocation is a way to ensure that everyone gets their location version.
themes: UX; geolocation; friction reduction; news apps
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Gregor Aisch (NYT): auto-geolocation removes friction vs. zip code entry

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: It is necessary to provide a global overview of the collection of data and, at the same time, let users analyze specific details or parts that they may judge as relevant to their goal.
themes: overview-detail; information architecture; user experience
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Mazza: overview + detail pattern for data visualization

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: On desktop, you can fill an article with lots of nice photos and little embeds, but I don't think that works quite as well on mobile because you can end up cluttering up a small screen and I think it loses some of the impacts that a single graphic has on mobile.
themes: mobile design; responsive; desktop vs mobile
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Elliot Bentley (WSJ): mobile needs fewer, higher-impact graphics

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Social media-oriented production is a production mode relying on interpersonal relationships, oriented to make users share, and aimed at embedding in users' online social life; in short, to adapt news production to social platforms rather than just using them. Letting users 'share' is not simply designing a share button on the interface, which may not necessarily be useful, but implementing the concept of 'sharing' in the whole production chain, so that the news itself has the potential to be shared.
chapter: Social Media-oriented Production of Data News
themes: social media; sharing; production workflow; distribution
relevance_rationale: Newsroom organization and collaboration patterns remain relevant regardless of AI tools
notes: Sharing must be embedded across entire production chain, not bolted on

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalism takes much longer to produce than traditional journalism because of the multiple components of data collection, analysis, and visualization. According to Google News Lab, 49% of data stories are created in a day or less, 30% take up to a week, 11% take several weeks, and 3% take a month or more. When selecting topics, data journalists must consider the production time of their work, so many focus on long-lasting topics rather than breaking news to extend the 'shelf life.'
chapter: The Long Production Time of Data Journalism Requires a Long Shelf Life
themes: production time; topic selection; timeliness; evergreen content
relevance_rationale: Newsroom organization and collaboration patterns remain relevant regardless of AI tools
notes: Google News Lab data on production timelines; evergreen topics fit DJ production cycles

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: At the information level, journalists need to identify the content that is most likely to spark the interest of users. The journalists should open with the most interesting or relevant fact or surprising data point. Include engaging movement or eye-catching colors as this will be the first thing people see on social media, and maybe the only thing if they scroll past too fast.
chapter: Sharing As the Core Production Strategy
themes: social media; lead design; visual hooks; engagement
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Segger: lead with most surprising data point; visual hook for scroll-past risk

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: At the formal level, data visualization designers should not pursue information in abundance, but rather make important data clearly identifiable. Alternatively, designers can let users gradually reveal the answers to questions that have been set up as they read the news. The Economist's team works to strip each chart down to make just one clear point so readers get the sense from scrolling through.
chapter: Sharing As the Core Production Strategy
themes: chart design; simplicity; one-point-per-chart; Economist
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Economist approach: one clear point per chart for social scroll context

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Lightweight design is about minimizing the technical or content load of data journalism without compromising the expression of meaning and user experience. Technical lightweighting means matching the technical form of data journalism products to the performance of mobile devices. Content lightweighting means that the amount of information should be based on the user's reading habits on social media platforms, which means that text should be short, graphics should be well focused, and video needs to tell the story in a short amount of time.
themes: mobile optimization; lightweight design; content economy
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Two dimensions: technical (loading, swipe vs click) and content (brevity, focus)

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: There are still many challenges to data news production, including untrained analysts, data reliability, measuring the right thing, and unjustified data collection. When published without context or consideration for ethics, data journalism can cause harm through perpetuating stereotypes and biases. The tools of data journalism can also be used to spread untruths, as data points are not facts; they are only given elements from which facts are derived.
themes: ethics; data quality; bias; stereotypes; context
relevance_rationale: Core data journalism insight persists regardless of AI tooling
notes: Kayser-Bril: data points are not facts but raw material for deriving facts

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Pragmatic objectivity consists of the objective stance and five criteria. The objective stance consists in moral virtues such as honesty and intellectual virtues such as caring about truth. It requires journalists to place a critical distance between oneself and the story, to be open to evidence and counter-arguments, to fairly represent other perspectives, and to be committed to the disinterested pursuit of truth for the public. The five criteria are: empirical validity, completeness and implications, consistency, self-consciousness, and intersubjective objectivity.
themes: pragmatic objectivity; verification criteria; Ward framework
relevance_rationale: Core data journalism insight persists regardless of AI tooling
notes: Ward's five-criteria framework for pragmatic objectivity in journalism

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: At the level of empirical validity, journalists need to systematically assess the quality of the data they collect. The main dimensions of evaluation include: First, the accuracy of the data -- journalists need to verify that the data is correct, cleaning formatting, spelling, and categorization errors. Second, the credibility of the data, including the credibility of the data source, the credibility compared to the usual internal standards, and the credibility based on the age of the data. Third, the appropriateness of the data -- journalists need to judge the amount of data necessary to solve a problem. Fourth, the scientificity of the data collection methods.
chapter: Principle of Objectivity in Data Collection
themes: data quality; accuracy; credibility; appropriateness; methodology
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Four-dimension data quality assessment: accuracy, credibility, appropriateness, scientificity

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Journalists must remain skeptical of data, 'unless it's coming from a really verified source, it's always important, especially when you're doing an original analysis of data, to be critical.' Usually data from authoritative sources (e.g., government, leading survey companies, reputable NGOs, and universities) can be trusted.
chapter: Principle of Objectivity in Data Collection
themes: source credibility; skepticism; verification; authoritative sources
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Bentley (WSJ): default skepticism unless source is verified authoritative

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: When collecting secondary data, it is important not to use only a single data set, but to collect from as many sources as possible, so that different data can corroborate each other. Simon Rogers believes that diverse data sources are essential for data journalism. At least two data sources should be included in data journalism production to demonstrate objectivity.
chapter: Principle of Objectivity in Data Collection
themes: data triangulation; multiple sources; corroboration
relevance_rationale: Data acquisition and cleaning workflows benefit from LLM-era tooling updates
notes: Rogers: minimum two data sources for objectivity; multi-source corroboration

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Sometimes when direct data is not available or incomplete for researching certain issues, journalists may use alternative data to conduct research, and then they need to consider what data is needed. The FiveThirtyEight website produced a data story examining trends in kidnapping cases in Nigeria. However, this conclusion is not true and the main reason for the error is that the journalist mistakenly equated the number of cases reported in the media with the number of cases that actually occurred.
chapter: Principle of Objectivity in Data Collection
themes: proxy data; data limitations; survivorship bias; measurement validity
relevance_rationale: Core data journalism insight persists regardless of AI tooling
notes: Caution: proxy data (media reports) != actual occurrence data

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The criterion of intersubjective objectivity requires journalists to communicate with data collectors and experts, to discuss all aspects of data collection, to listen to more opinions and suggestions from different parties. A Miami Herald reporter's story about judges not punishing some DUI offenders turned out to be inaccurate because the reporter did not have knowledge of some of the special circumstances listed when he obtained the data.
chapter: Principle of Objectivity in Data Collection
themes: expert consultation; domain knowledge; intersubjective verification
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Consult domain experts to understand data context and special codes

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: While the results of data analysis may seem fair and objective, the values of journalists actually influence the process of data construction to interpretation. Data does not intrinsically imply truth. Yes, we can find truth in data, through a process of honest inference. But we can also find and argue multiple truths or even outright falsehoods from data.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Analysis
themes: data interpretation; values; honest inference; multiple truths
relevance_rationale: Core data journalism insight persists regardless of AI tooling
notes: Gittleman and Diakopoulos: data does not intrinsically imply truth

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: WNYC's 'Median Income Across the US' uses medians to present income distributions, a simplistic treatment that leads to data maps that differ from the actual situation. Some of the green areas are actually the poorest areas of the city, due to the fact that people in some of these poor areas share an apartment with more than 10 people, thus driving up household income, but obviously this does not mean that the area is affluent.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Analysis
themes: statistical measures; medians; misleading aggregation; ecological fallacy
relevance_rationale: Core data journalism insight persists regardless of AI tooling
notes: Median household income can mislead when living arrangements vary across areas

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The self-consciousness criterion requires journalists to have a thorough knowledge of data analysis methods and to maintain a critical attitude. Specifically, journalists need to understand the original context of the data they collect, the strengths and limitations of the data analysis methods they choose, and the problems that may arise in answering particular questions.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Analysis
themes: method awareness; limitations; critical thinking
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Know context of data, strengths/limitations of method, and potential pitfalls

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The criterion of intersubjective objectivity requires journalists to communicate and discuss data analysis methods and their conclusions with experts and peers, rather than making their own decisions alone, and refining data analysis and conclusions by listening to different opinions. Data itself is a simplification of things, and with simplification comes the danger of distortion. Therefore, the role of experts is very important in the data analysis section.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Analysis
themes: peer review; expert consultation; collaborative analysis
relevance_rationale: Newsroom organization and collaboration patterns remain relevant regardless of AI tools
notes: Never analyze alone; consult experts because simplification risks distortion

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Common inaccuracies in data visualizations include data sizes that are out of proportion to the area in the data visualization, and pie charts whose parts add up to more than 100%. Simon Rogers argues that aesthetic deficiencies and design flaws in data visualization are excusable, but factual distortions and misinformation are inexcusable.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Visualization
themes: visualization accuracy; pie charts; proportions; factual distortion
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Rogers: aesthetic flaws forgivable, factual distortion not

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: The New York Times made a distorted map, turning all the states into squares of varying sizes according to the number of electoral votes so that the comparison of the two candidates' votes would be more visual and accurate. The consistency criterion requires that data visualization follow design conventions and conform to people's perceptions of visual symbols in a certain period of time and in a certain cultural context.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Visualization
themes: cartogram; design conventions; electoral maps; proportional representation
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: NYT used cartogram to fix area-distortion; follow cultural design conventions

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Reuters has published a graphic showing a dramatic spike in Florida murders by firearm after the Stand Your Ground law was enacted in 2005. However, this graph gives the impression that shootings are down after the Act was implemented because the Y-axis values grow from top to bottom instead of the generic bottom-to-top.
chapter: The Principle of Objectivity in Data Visualization
themes: Y-axis inversion; misleading charts; design conventions; Florida gun deaths
relevance_rationale: AI/automation landscape has evolved significantly since this was written
notes: Inverted Y-axis reverses perceived trend; violates bottom-to-top convention

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Data journalists should allow others to replicate their work, and therefore need to publish their research methods just like scientists do. For data journalists, making data, data analysis, and algorithms transparent can prompt them to review their operational processes, aware that an external audience is monitoring them. Transparency benefits the computing field and the community of data journalism, and the constructive feedback that a high-level audience will provide can improve the accuracy of their work.
themes: transparency; replicability; open methods; accountability
relevance_rationale: AI/automation landscape has evolved significantly since this was written
notes: Scientific model: publish methods so work is replicable and externally auditable

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Transparency in data collection means that data reporters should publish information related to the data (e.g., specific sources of data, evaluation of data quality, tools and processes used to collect the data, etc.) and provide links to the raw data or data. The transparency of data sources means that if someone wants to verify the accuracy and veracity of the data, they should be able to find the original data or the source at the lowest possible cost.
themes: data transparency; source disclosure; raw data access; verification cost
relevance_rationale: Data acquisition and cleaning workflows benefit from LLM-era tooling updates
notes: Publish source, quality assessment, tools, and links to raw data

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Transparency in data collection also involves an account of data quality. Sometimes the data used by journalists may even be flawed. Even data used in scientific research has limitations, so journalists need to make those limitations clear so that audiences can judge the extent to which they can trust the story.
themes: data limitations; quality disclosure; audience trust
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Disclose data limitations so audience can calibrate trust

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Algorithmic transparency requires an accounting of the use of data and how the algorithm is involved in the news production process. The algorithms have a strong influence on the extraction of data, discourse analysis, and interpretation of results, because they are human 'creations,' meaning that multiple human factors, including the creator's intent, are embedded in the algorithms.
themes: algorithmic transparency; human bias in algorithms; accountability
relevance_rationale: AI/automation landscape has evolved significantly since this was written
notes: Algorithms embed creator intent; transparency means disclosing how they shape results

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: According to Nicholas Diakopoulos, algorithmic transparency consists of five specific aspects: (1) the criteria used to prioritize, rank, emphasize, or editorialize things in the algorithm; (2) what data act as inputs to the algorithm; (3) the accuracy including the false positive and false negative rate of errors; (4) descriptions of training data and its potential bias; and (5) the definitions, operationalizations, or thresholds used by similarity or classification algorithms.
themes: algorithmic transparency; Diakopoulos framework; error rates; training data
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Five-aspect algorithmic transparency checklist from Diakopoulos

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: BuzzFeed published the raw data, algorithmic procedures, and analysis process of 'The Tennis Racket,' a collaboration with the BBC, on GitHub. Although BuzzFeed stated in advance that the results of the analysis were not evidence that the player had faked the game and anonymized the data, those familiar with the field could easily find out who the suspected faker was based on the full data and analysis process provided by BuzzFeed. Transparency in this case is a bit 'excessive,' and there should be limits to transparency.
themes: transparency limits; privacy; anonymization failure; harm
relevance_rationale: AI/automation landscape has evolved significantly since this was written
notes: Full transparency can harm individuals even with anonymization; limits needed

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Transparency in data visualization means linking news sources in the visualization as much as possible, fully explaining any caveats related to the data or to the chart itself, and informing the wider public of the danger signs to watch out for before spreading what may turn out to be misinformation. If a data visualization designer uses an unconventional design, the audience needs to be reminded to avoid understanding it in a conventional way.
themes: visualization transparency; caveats; source linking; unconventional design
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Burn-Murdoch: link sources, explain caveats, flag unconventional designs

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
extract: Correction transparency means that when a data journalism piece is significantly flawed, the media must explain the reasons for the error and apologize to the audience. The media also needs to make transparent some other details in data journalism production that should be explained to the audience, such as the motivation for the selection of the topic, the problems encountered in the interview, and how they were solved.
themes: corrections; error handling; editorial transparency; motivation disclosure
relevance_rationale: Core journalistic procedure remains essential regardless of tooling
notes: Disclose corrections with explanations, topic motivation, and production challenges

source_title: The Power of Data: Data Journalism Production and Ethics
author: Zhang Chao
